projects.title: CRM Digital FTE — Omnichannel AI Customer Success Agent
projects.description: Autonomous AI customer success agent handling Gmail, WhatsApp, and Web Form simultaneously. Built with OpenAI Agents SDK, FastAPI, PostgreSQL with pgvector, Kafka event streaming, and deployed on Kubernetes with auto-scaling.
projects.problem: A SaaS company needs 24/7 customer support across 3 channels but human FTEs cost $75,000/year. This Digital FTE operates at under $1,000/year.
projects.solution: MCP-powered agent with 5 specialized tools, cross-channel customer identity resolution, sentiment-based escalation, and production Kubernetes deployment.
highlights: Built custom PostgreSQL CRM with pgvector
Kafka event streaming for decoupled channel processing
MCP Server exposing 5 tools to the agent
HPA auto-scaling: 3-30 pods based on load

projects.title: Todo App → Cloud-Native AI Agent (Spec-Driven Evolution)
projects.description: 5-phase evolution from a simple Python CLI todo list to a full cloud-native AI agent on Kubernetes. Demonstrates spec-driven development methodology from first principles to production.
projects.problem: Show a complete software evolution journey — from console CLI to AI-powered app to Kubernetes-deployed microservices.
projects.solution: 5 progressive phases: CLI → Web App → AI Chatbot Interface → Local Kubernetes + HF Spaces with Kafka and Dapr.
metrics: Local Kubernetes on Minikube; production backend on Hugging Face Spaces
Vercel auto-deploy on push to main; backend deployed via HF Space
highlights: Natural language interface: 'Reschedule morning meetings to 2 PM'
Real-time browser notifications — in-app toast + OS push on reminder tasks, powered by DB polling

projects.title: Personal AI Employee — Autonomous Digital FTE
projects.description: A 24/7 autonomous AI agent managing personal and business affairs using Claude Code + Obsidian. Monitors Gmail and WhatsApp, processes incoming messages, generates weekly business reports, and handles routine tasks with human-in-the-loop safety.
projects.problem: Repetitive personal and business tasks consume hours weekly. A human VA costs $4,000-$8,000/month and only works 40 hours/week.
projects.solution: Local-first autonomous agent with Python watchers, MCP servers, HITL approval workflow, and weekly CEO briefing reports.
projects.demo_note: Local deployment — GitHub repo + architecture walkthrough available
highlights: Watchers pattern for Gmail and WhatsApp monitoring

projects.title: Physical AI & Humanoid Robotics Textbook
projects.description: Panaversity Hackathon I submission: a production-grade AI-native textbook platform teaching Physical AI and Humanoid Robotics across 13 weeks. Covers ROS 2, Gazebo, NVIDIA Isaac Sim, and a Conversational Humanoid capstone. Built with Docusaurus v3, FastAPI, Neon PostgreSQL, Qdrant Cloud, LangChain, and Groq LLaMA 3.3 70B.
projects.problem: Physical AI — AI systems that operate in the real physical world — is the next frontier after LLMs, yet no structured, accessible textbook exists for it. Existing robotics courses are scattered across dozens of resources, assume identical hardware for every learner, and offer zero in-context AI help when a student gets stuck. The challenge is compounded by three simultaneously heavy computational domains: Physics Simulation (Isaac Sim / Gazebo), Visual Perception (SLAM / Computer Vision), and Generative AI (LLMs). A student with an RTX 4080 workstation and one with a Jetson Orin Nano edge kit need entirely different guidance — but every existing resource gives them the same content. Pakistani learners face an additional barrier: dense English-only material with no mechanism to get real-time clarification in their native language.
projects.solution: A full-stack AI-native textbook platform with four progressive modules: (1) ROS 2 — the Robotic Nervous System, covering nodes, topics, services, URDF, and Python-ROS controllers; (2) Gazebo & Unity — physics simulation with LiDAR, depth cameras, and IMUs; (3) NVIDIA Isaac — photorealistic sim, Isaac ROS, hardware-accelerated VSLAM, Nav2 path planning, and sim-to-real RL; (4) Conversational Humanoid capstone — integrating LLMs for natural language robot interaction. Platform features: RAG chatbot built with LangChain + Qdrant + Groq LLaMA 3.3 70B with FastEmbed (any highlighted text → Ask AI instantly); hardware-aware personalization at signup across 3 rig profiles (RTX Sim Workstation / Jetson Edge Kit / Unitree G1 Robot); one-click Roman Urdu chapter translation; JWT auth with 3-step hardware onboarding; admin dashboard for curriculum ingestion and Qdrant reindexing.
metrics: 13-week curriculum across 4 modules (ROS 2 → Gazebo → Isaac → Conversational Humanoid)
180 vectors indexed in Qdrant Cloud for RAG across all 13 weeks
12-table Neon PostgreSQL schema (users, profiles, sessions, curriculum)
3 hardware profiles with fully personalized content paths
All hackathon bonus deliverables completed: Auth + Personalization + Urdu Translation
highlights: RAG chatbot with LangChain + Qdrant: highlight any text → Ask AI for instant clarification
Hardware-aware onboarding at signup: content adapts to RTX workstation, Jetson edge kit, or Unitree G1
One-click Roman Urdu translation for every chapter — built for Pakistani learners
Admin dashboard with curriculum ingestion pipeline and Qdrant reindexing
Full CI/CD: GitHub Actions auto-deploys to GitHub Pages on every push
